The questionnaire contains 7 functional system; pyramidal functions, cerebellar functions, brainstem functions, sensory function, bowel and bladder function, visual function, and cerebral (or mental) functions. The functional system (FS) scores collected from this questionnaire are used in conjunction with observations and information regarding ambulation and the use of assistive devices to rate the EDSS.

instrument_label: Karnofsky Performance Status Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Karnofsky Performance Status Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale originally developed by David A. Karnofsky and Joseph H. Burchenal in 1949 which is used to evaluate the degree to which a patient is functionally impaired. This instrument consists of 3 sections, no special care needed, unable to work, and unable to care for one's self and is completed by a clinician based on their observations. There are a total of eleven levels ranging from 0-100 where 0 indicates that the patient is dead and 100 indicates that the patient is normal.

instrument_label: Life Events Checklist for DSM-5 Standard Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Life Events Checklist for DSM-5 Standard Version Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 17-item self-report measure, published in 2013, that is used to investigate exposure to potentially traumatic events that meet diagnostic criterion A for post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) according to the Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-5). The instrument is used to assess exposure to 16 event categories known to potentially result in PTSD or distress, as well as an additional item addressing any other extraordinarily stressful events not captured by the 16 categories, in adults 18 years of age and older.

instrument_label: Modified Cigarette Evaluation Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Modified Cigarette Evaluation Questionnaire
instrument_definition: The modified version of a standardized 12-item self-report questionnaire, published by Cappelleri et al in 2007, that utilizes a seven-point rating scale to assess the effects of smoking cigarettes from the perspective of smokers.

instrument_label: Montreal Classification for Crohn's Disease Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Montreal Classification for Crohn's Disease Clinical Classification
container_name: Montreal Classification for Crohn's Disease Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized classification system adapted from the Vienne Classification in 2008 that stratifies patients with Crohn disease based on age at diagnosis, extent of disease, and disease behavior over time.

instrument_label: Model for End Stage Liver Disease Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Model for End Stage Liver Disease Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Malinchoc et al in 2000, which is a classification system used to assess severity of chronic liver disease and to determine the urgency of a subject needing a liver transplant within the next three months. This instrument uses the values of serum bilirubin and creatinine, and the international normalized ratio for prothrombin time (INR) to predict survival for patients with advanced liver disease.

instrument_label: Model for End Stage Liver Disease - Serum Na 2008 Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Model for End Stage Liver Disease - Serum Na 2008 Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized scoring system, published by Kim et al. in 2008, that combines the Model for End-Stage Liver Disease (MELD) score with serum sodium (Na) concentration to assess disease severity and increase the accuracy of predicting mortality in patients with end-stage liver disease.

instrument_label: Modified Fatigue Impact Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Modified Fatigue Impact Scale Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Fisk et al in 1994, which assesses how fatigue may affect a person and the impact of fatigue on quality of life. The questionnaire contains 21 questions which are scored ranging from 0 (never) to 4 (almost always).

instrument_label: Modified Hachinski Ischemic Scale-NACC Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Modified Hachinski Ischemic Scale-NACC Version Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale originally developed by Hachinski et al in 1975 and later modified by Rosen et al in 1980 to clinically differentiate two most common types of dementia: senile dementia of the Alzheimer type (SDAT), a primary degenerative disease, and primary degenerative disease (MID), a vascular disorder. The modified version of the questionnaire contains 8 features for more accurate diagnosis of MID, the vascular dementia type. This questionnaire can be scored between 0 and 18, where a score greater than 7 suggests vascular involvement.

instrument_label: Modified Medical Research Council Dyspnea Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Modified Medical Research Council Dyspnea Scale Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Mahler and Wells in 1988, which is a general scale used to measure the rating of dyspnea or breathlessness. The questionnaire contains 1 question and a 0-4 grade answer scale, where grade 0 represents the lowest level of dyspnea impairment perceived, and level 4 represents the greatest dyspnea impairment.

instrument_label: Mini-Mental State Examination 2 Standard Version Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Mini-Mental State Examination 2 Standard Version Functional Test
container_name: MMSE-2 Standard Version Functional Test Question
instrument_definition: An updated version of the Mini-Mental State Examination, a brief, quantitative measure of cognitive status in adults, that was originally developed by Marshal Folstein, Susan Folstein and Paul McHugh (Journal of Psychiatric Research, 1975), where the overall difficulty and raw score range remain the same despite modifications to some tasks and replacement of some items.

instrument_label: Michigan Neuropathy Screening Instrument Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Michigan Neuropathy Screening Instrument Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized survey developed and copyrighted by the University of Michigan's Diabetes Research and Training Center and is based on Peter Dyck's Neuropathy Screening Profile (Dyck PJ, Karnes J, O'Brien PC, Swanson CJ, Neuropathy Symptom Profile in health, motor neuron disease, diabetic neuropathy, and amyloidosis. Neurology, 1986, 36:1300-1308) which is used to screen for neuropathy particularly in individuals with diabetes. This instrument consists of two parts, the first part is a patient history and can be completed by the patient while the second part is physical exam completed by a health professional.

instrument_label: Mayo-Portland Adaptability Inventory-4 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Mayo-Portland Adaptability Inventory-4 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale modified and revised by Muriel et al in 2005, which is used to measure global brain injury outcome and functional status of subjects in the post-acute period following acquired brain injury (ABI)/traumatic brain injury (TBI), as well as to evaluate rehabilitation programs providing services to people with brain injuries. The questionnaire contains 35 questions and generates 3 subscales: ability index, adjustment index, and participation index. This instrument is scored on a 5-point Likert scale.

instrument_label: Modified Rankin Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Modified Rankin Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized clinical classification originally developed by John Rankin in 1957, and later modified by Van Swieten et al in 1988, which is used to assess the degree of disability or dependence in the daily activities of individuals who have suffered a stroke. It also allows comparison between subjects with different kinds of neurologic deficits. The questionnaire contains a '0 to 6' scale, ranging from 'no symptoms at all' (scored 0) to 'dead' (scored 6).

instrument_label: Multiple Sclerosis Quality of Life-54 Instrument Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Multiple Sclerosis Quality of Life-54 Instrument Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Vickrey et al in 1995 and 1997, which combines items that are generic and disease-specific to assess quality of life and the level of physical, emotional, and cognitive functions in patients with multiple sclerosis. The questionnaire contains 54 questions and generates 12 subscales: physical function, role limitations due to physical problems, role limitations due to emotional problems, pain, emotional well-being, energy, health perceptions, social function, cognitive function, health distress, sexual function and overall quality of life.

instrument_label: Minnesota Tobacco Withdrawal Scale-Revised Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Minnesota Tobacco Withdrawal Scale-Revised Questionnaire
container_name: Minnesota Tobacco Withdrawal Scale-Revised Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 17-item assessment tool, published by Hughes et al in 2005, that utilizes a five-point rating scale to assess the effects of smoking cessation, including withdrawal and other symptoms, in a single total score.

instrument_label: Modified Van Assche Index Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Modified Van Assche Index Clinical Classification
container_name: Modified Van Assche Index Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized scoring system originally developed in 2003 and modified in 2017 that utilizes quantitative radiological evaluation to assess the severity and disease response in perianal fistulizing Crohn disease.

instrument_label: National Early Warning Score 2 Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: National Early Warning Score 2 Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A 2017 update to the Royal College of Physicians of London's 2012 National Early Warning Score (NEWS), which is a weighted scoring system for six routinely monitored vital sign parameters, including respiration rate, oxygen saturation, systolic blood pressure, pulse rate, level of consciousness, and temperature, to standardize the assessment and response to acute illness. NEWS2 includes several modifications to the vital sign weightings and a new SpO2 scoring scale for patients at risk of type II respiratory failure.

instrument_label: Negative Symptom Assessment-16 Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Negative Symptom Assessment-16 Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Axelrod et al in 1993, which is a classification system used to assess the presence, severity, and various negative symptoms associated with schizophrenia. This instrument contains 16 items and the characterization of negative symptoms is based on a 5-factor model: communication, emotion/affect, social involvement, motivation, and retardation.

instrument_label: North Star Ambulatory Assessment Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: North Star Ambulatory Assessment Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized 17-item scale that was developed by Elaine Scott and Michelle Eagle in 2011 to measure functional motor ability in ambulant children and adolescents with Duchenne muscular dystrophy. The instrument, which is partly based on the Hammersmith Motor Ability Scale (2003), utilizes a 3-point rating scale to grade activities ranging from standing to running, as well as abilities that are necessary for a patient to remain functionally ambulant and that are known to progressively deteriorate in untreated DMD.

instrument_label: The Oswestry Disability Index Version 2.1a Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: The Oswestry Disability Index Version 2.1a Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized survey initially developed by John O'Brien in 1976 then published in 1980 by Jeremy Fairbank. This version (2.1a) is copyrighted by Jeremy Fairbank and is used in the assessment and management of spinal disorders. There are 10 items pertaining to a variety of topics including personal care, walking, standing, sleeping and traveling. Each item has six responses.

instrument_label: Observer Global Impression Generic Modification Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Observer Global Impression Generic Modification Version Questionnaire
container_name: Observer Global Impression Generic Modification Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A modified version of a standardized questionnaire published in 1976 by Guy et al. that was initially created to provide an observer-determined summary measure of a patient's global functioning prior to and after initiating a study medication. It has been modified for use in any disease/condition concept use case.

instrument_label: PROMIS Item Bank v1.0 - Sleep Disturbance - Short Form 4a Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: PROMIS Item Bank v1.0 - Sleep Disturbance - Short Form 4a Questionnaire
container_name: PROMIS Item Bank v1.0 - Sleep Disturbance - Short Form 4a Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: The first version of a standardized four-item subset of the PROMIS Sleep Disturbance item bank, published by Yu et al. in 2011, that utilizes a five-point rating scale to assess self-reported perceptions of sleep quality and depth, as well as restoration associated with sleep.

instrument_label: Positive and Negative Syndrome Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Positive and Negative Syndrome Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Kay et al in 1987, which is a classification system used to measure severity of patients with schizophrenia. This instrument rates a patient on a scale of 1 (absent) to 7 (extreme) on 30 different symptoms.

instrument_label: The PTSD Checklist for DSM-5 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: The PTSD Checklist for DSM-5 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 20-item self-report measure, published in 2013, that is used to assess the 20 Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders (DSM-5) symptoms of post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), using a 5-point Likert-type intensity scale, ranging from zero (not at all) to four (extremely).

instrument_label: Perianal Crohn's Disease Activity Index Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Perianal Crohn's Disease Activity Index Questionnaire
container_name: Perianal Crohn's Disease Activity Index Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized five-item questionnaire, published by Irvine et al. in 1995, that utilizes a five-point rating scale to assess the severity and complexity of five frequent features of Crohn's disease affecting the perianal area.

instrument_label: Patient Determined Disease Steps Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Patient Determined Disease Steps Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Hohol et al in 1995, which is a self-assessment scale of functional disability in multiple sclerosis patients primarily based on ambulation. The questionnaire contains 1 question which is scored ranging from 0 (normal) to 8 (bedridden). A score of 0 to 2 indicates mild disability; a score of 3 to 5 indicates moderate disability; a score of 6 to 8 indicates severe disability.

instrument_label: Patient Global Impression Generic Modification Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Patient Global Impression Generic Modification Version Questionnaire
container_name: PGI Generic Modification Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A modified, generic version of a standardized questionnaire, developed as a patient reported outcomes counterpart for the Clinical Global Impressions scale published by Guy et al. in 1976, that assesses a patient's perception of their condition.

instrument_label: Pediatric Outcomes Data Collection Instrument, Pediatric Parent-Reported Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Pediatric Outcomes Data Collection Instrument, Pediatric Parent-Reported Version Questionnaire
container_name: PODCI Pediatric Parent-Reported Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 86-item parent-report questionnaire, developed by the American Academy of Orthopaedic Surgeons and the Pediatric Orthopaedic Society of North America (POSNA) in 1998 to assess the overall health, pain, and ability to participate in normal daily activities in patients 10 years of age or younger. The instrument includes five scales: upper extremity and physical function, transfer and mobility tasks, sports/physical functioning, pain/comfort, treatment expectations, happiness, and satisfaction with symptoms, as well as a Global Functioning scale that consists of the mean of the values for the first four scales.

instrument_label: Pediatric Outcomes Data Collection Instrument, Adolescent Parent-Reported Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Pediatric Outcomes Data Collection Instrument, Adolescent Parent-Reported Version Questionnaire
container_name: PODCI Adolescent Parent-Reported Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 86-item parent-report questionnaire, developed by the American Academy of Orthopaedic Surgeons and the Pediatric Orthopaedic Society of North America (POSNA) in 1998 to assess the overall health, pain, and ability to participate in normal daily activities in patients 11 to 18 years of age. The instrument includes five scales: upper extremity and physical function, transfer and mobility tasks, sports/physical functioning, pain/comfort, treatment expectations, happiness, and satisfaction with symptoms, as well as a Global Functioning scale that consists of the mean of the values for the first four scales.

instrument_label: Pediatric Outcomes Data Collection Instrument, Adolescent Self-Reported Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Pediatric Outcomes Data Collection Instrument, Adolescent Self-Reported Version Questionnaire
container_name: PODCI Adolescent Self-Reported Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 83-item self-report questionnaire, developed by the American Academy of Orthopaedic Surgeons and the Pediatric Orthopaedic Society of North America (POSNA) in 1998 to assess the overall health, pain, and ability to participate in normal daily activities in patients 11 to 18 years of age. The instrument includes five scales: upper extremity and physical function, transfer and mobility tasks, sports/physical functioning, pain/comfort, treatment expectations, happiness, and satisfaction with symptoms, as well as a Global Functioning scale that consists of the mean of the values for the first four scales.

instrument_label: Penn State Electronic Cigarette Dependence Index Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Penn State Electronic Cigarette Dependence Index Questionnaire
container_name: Penn State Electronic Cigarette Dependence Index Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 10-item questionnaire, published by Foulds et al. in 2011 and adapted from the Penn State Nicotine Dependence Index, that assesses e-cigarette dependence.

instrument_label: Patient-Reported Outcomes Version of the Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events Item Library Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Patient-Reported Outcomes Version of the Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events Item Library Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: The first version of a database containing 124 questions that represent 78 symptomatic adverse events taken from the Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events.

instrument_label: Quick Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology Clinician-Rated Version Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Quick Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology Clinician-Rated Version Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized 16-item clinician-scored questionnaire, derived from the 30-item Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology (IDS), that utilizes a 5-point rating scale for the assessment of the severity of depressive symptoms over the seven day period prior to evaluation. The original questionnaire, created by Rush et al. in 1986, includes the nine diagnostic DSM-IV symptom domains that are used to characterize a major depressive episode, as well as items to address melancholic and atypical features. The shortened, 16-item version was initially developed by Rush et al. in 2000 by selecting only items from the original scale that assessed DSM-IV criterion diagnostic symptoms.

instrument_label: Quick Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology Self-Report Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Quick Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology Self-Report Version Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 16-item self-report questionnaire, derived from the 30-item Inventory of Depressive Symptomatology (IDS), that utilizes a 5-point rating scale for the assessment of the severity of depressive symptoms over the seven day period prior to evaluation. The original questionnaire, created by Rush et al. in 1986, includes the nine diagnostic DSM-IV symptom domains that are used to characterize a major depressive episode, as well as items to address melancholic and atypical features. The shortened, 16-item version was initially developed by Rush et al. in 2000 by selecting only items from the original scale that assessed DSM-IV criterion diagnostic symptoms.

instrument_label: Quality Of Life Enjoyment And Satisfaction Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Quality Of Life Enjoyment And Satisfaction Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized self-report questionnaire, available as a 16-item short form for adults and 93-item long form for children, adolescents, and adults, that utilizes a 5-point rating scale to assess the degree of enjoyment and satisfaction experienced by individuals in various areas of daily functioning. The questionnaire was developed by Endicot et al. in 1993 to assess broad functional impairment in research and treatment settings.

instrument_label: RAND 36-Item Health Survey 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: RAND 36-Item Health Survey 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Hays et al in 1993 and the RAND corporation as part of the Medical Outcome Study, which is a generic measurement of health-related quality of life. This questionnaire contains 36 questions and generates 8 subscales: physical functioning, bodily pain, role limitations due to physical health problems, role limitations due to personal or emotional problems, general mental health, social functioning, energy/fatigue, and general health perceptions.

instrument_label: Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized 10-point rating scale, developed by Sessler et al. 2002, that is used to assess the level of alertness and agitation in critically ill patients. The assigned score ranges from +4 to -5, with four levels of anxiety or agitation, a score of zero denoting a calm and alert state, and five levels of sedation.

instrument_label: Reynolds Cardiovascular Disease 10-Year Risk Score Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Reynolds Cardiovascular Disease 10-Year Risk Score Clinical Classification
container_name: Reynolds CVD 10-Year Risk Score Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed and modified by Ridker et al in 2007 and 2008, which is a classification system used to estimate the 10-year risk of cardiovascular disease (CVD). This estimation takes into consideration of an individual's sex, age, smoking and CVD family histories, as well as systolic blood pressure, high sensitivity C-reactive protein (hsCRP), total cholesterol and high-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol levels.

instrument_label: Rockport One Mile Walk Test Version 1.0 Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Rockport One Mile Walk Test Version 1.0 Functional Test
instrument_definition: A submaximal fitness test, developed by Kline et al. in 1987, that utilizes the fastest time taken to walk one mile (T1), heart rate measured at the fourth quarter of the mile (HR1-4), sex (SEX; male = 1, female = 0), age in years (AGE), and body weight in pounds (WT) to estimate maximal oxygen consumption (VO2max) using the equation: VO2max = 6.9652 + (0.0091*WT) - (0.0257*AGE) + (0.5955*SEX) - (0.2240*T1) - (0.0115*HR1-4). IMPORTANT NOTICE: The NCI Thesaurus contains biomedical terminologies that NCI does not own or control. This concept contains gender-related content that does not comply with Executive Order 14168.

instrument_label: RAND Social Support Survey Instrument Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: RAND Social Support Survey Instrument Questionnaire
container_name: RAND Social Support Survey Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 19-item survey developed by Cathy Donalds Helbourne and Anita L. Stewart in 1991 for the assessment of recent thinking about the various dimensions of social support in patients with chronic conditions in the Medical Outcomes Study (MOS). The instrument measures five dimensions of social support: emotional support, informational support, tangible support, positive social interaction, and affectionate support.

instrument_label: Rutgeerts Score Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Rutgeerts Score Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized scoring system developed by Rutgeerts et al. in 1990 that is used to assess postoperative disease recurrence in patients that have undergone ileocecal resection as treatment for Crohn disease.

instrument_label: Riker Sedation-Agitation Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Riker Sedation-Agitation Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized seven-point rating scale developed by Riker et al. 1999 to assess the level of alertness and agitation in critically ill patients. The assigned score ranges from 1 to 7, with three levels of sedation ranging from unarousable to sedated, one level indicating calm and cooperative, and three levels of agitation, ranging from agitated to dangerous agitation.

instrument_label: Sheehan Disability Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Sheehan Disability Scale Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized questionnaire developed by David Sheehan in 1983, which is used to measure functional impairments caused by panic, anxiety, phobic, or depressive symptoms in three inter-related domains; work/school, social and family life. It is also used in many other chronic medical illnesses due to its generic design. The Sheehan Disability Scale generates 4 scores: a work disability score, a social life disability score, a family life disability score and a total score. This questionnaire contains 6 items, with which patients can rate the extent of their functional impairments on a 10-point visual analog scale.

instrument_label: Simple Endoscopic Score for Crohn's Disease Version 1 Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Simple Endoscopic Score for Crohn's Disease Version 1 Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized endoscopic scoring system that was developed as a simplified alternative to the Crohn's Disease Endoscopic Index of Severity (CDEIS) by Daperno et al. 2004. The presence and size of ulcerations and the extent of the inflammatory area and stenosis are assessed in each of the five ileocolonic segments (rectum, sigmoid and left colon, transverse colon, right colon, and ileum) and given a severity score ranging from zero to three, and the cumulative score is determined, with higher scores indicating severe disease.

instrument_label: Short Form 10 Health Survey for Children Standard, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short Form 10 Health Survey for Children Standard, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
container_name: SF10 v1.0 Children Standard Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 10-item parent-report survey, adapted from the Child Health Questionnaire in 2003, that utilizes a four week recall of eight domains of physical and psychosocial functioning to assess health-related quality of life in the pediatric population.

instrument_label: Short Form 10 Health Survey for Children Acute, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short Form 10 Health Survey for Children Acute, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
container_name: SF10 v1.0 Children Acute Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 10-item parent-report survey, adapted from the Child Health Questionnaire in 2003, that utilizes a one week recall of eight domains of physical and psychosocial functioning to assess health-related quality of life in the pediatric population.

instrument_label: Short Form 12 Health Survey Standard, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short Form 12 Health Survey Standard, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 12-item self-report questionnaire, adapted from the Medical Outcomes Study 36-Item Short-Form Health Survey (SF-36) in 1996, that utilizes a four week recall of eight domains of physical and psychosocial functioning to assess the impact of health on an individual's everyday life.

instrument_label: Short Form 12 Health Survey Acute, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short Form 12 Health Survey Acute, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 12-item self-report questionnaire, adapted from the Medical Outcomes Study 36-Item Short-Form Health Survey (SF-36) in 1996, that utilizes a one week recall of eight domains of physical and psychosocial functioning to assess the impact of health on an individual's everyday life.

instrument_label: Short Form 12 Health Survey Standard, US Version 2.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short Form 12 Health Survey Standard, US Version 2.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 12-item self-report questionnaire, originally adapted from the Medical Outcomes Study 36-Item Short-Form Health Survey (SF-36) in 1996 and revised in 2002, that utilizes a four week recall of eight domains of physical and psychosocial functioning to assess the impact of health on an individual's everyday life.

instrument_label: Short Form 12 Health Survey Acute, US Version 2.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short Form 12 Health Survey Acute, US Version 2.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 12-item self-report questionnaire, originally adapted from the Medical Outcomes Study 36-Item Short-Form Health Survey (SF-36) in 1996 and revised in 2002, that utilizes a one week recall of eight domains of physical and psychosocial functioning to assess the impact of health on an individual's everyday life.

instrument_label: SF-6Dv2 Health Utility Survey Standard, English Version 2.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: SF-6Dv2 Health Utility Survey Standard, English Version 2.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized stand-alone six-item patient report questionnaire derived from the SF-6D that assesses a range of symptoms, experiences, and health outcomes in six health domains over the past four weeks in order to capture changes across physical and mental health conditions and produce a single health utility score.

instrument_label: SF-6Dv2 Health Utility Survey Acute, English Version 2.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: SF-6Dv2 Health Utility Survey Acute, English Version 2.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized stand-alone six-item patient report questionnaire derived from the SF-6D that assesses a range of symptoms, experiences, and health outcomes in six health domains over the past week in order to capture changes across physical and mental health conditions and produce a single health utility score.

instrument_label: Short Form 8 Health Survey Standard, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short Form 8 Health Survey Standard, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized eight-item short version of the Medical Outcomes Study 36-Item Short-Form Health Survey (SF-36) that utilizes a five-point rating scale and a four week recall to assess health-related quality of life. The measured dimensions include general health, physical functioning, role-physical, bodily pain, vitality, social functioning, mental health, and role-emotional.

instrument_label: Short Form 8 Health Survey Acute, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short Form 8 Health Survey Acute, US Version 1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized eight-item short version of the Medical Outcomes Study 36-Item Short-Form Health Survey (SF-36) that utilizes a five-point rating scale and a one week recall to assess health-related quality of life. The measured dimensions include general health, physical functioning, role-physical, bodily pain, vitality, social functioning, mental health, and role-emotional.

instrument_label: Suicidal Ideation Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Suicidal Ideation Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 30-item self-report questionnaire developed by William M. Reynolds in 1987 that utilizes a seven-point rating scale to assess the frequency of suicidal thoughts in adolescents grades 10 to 12.

instrument_label: 6 Minute Walk Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: 6 Minute Walk Functional Test 2008 Version
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Bruno Blake in 1963, which is a performance-based evaluation of functional exercise capacity in subjects with chronic respiratory disease and heart failure, as well as other populations such as healthy older adults and people suffering from fibromyalgia and scleroderma. This functional test contains 6 items and measures the distance an individual is able to walk over a total of six minutes on a hard, flat surface.

instrument_label: Symptoms of Major Depressive Disorder Scale v1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Symptoms of Major Depressive Disorder Scale v1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 35-item patient-reported outcome instrument, created by McCarrier et al. in 2015, that utilizes a 7-day recall period and a five-point verbal rating scale to assess symptoms of major depressive disorder.

instrument_label: Subjective Opiate Withdrawal Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Subjective Opiate Withdrawal Scale Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Leonard Handelsman and colleagues in 1987 to evaluate the severity of an individual's opiate withdrawal signs and symptoms. This instrument contains 16 items or symptoms that pertain to opiate withdrawal, which are completed by the individual. Each item is rated on a scale of 0 to 4 with 0 being not at all and 4 being extremely.

instrument_label: Short Physical Performance Battery Version 1.2 Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Short Physical Performance Battery Version 1.2 Functional Test
instrument_definition: A standardized five-item 10-minute assessment tool, developed by Guralnik et al. 1994, that is used for the evaluation of lower extremity physical function and consists of three subtests, including standing balance, walking, and rising from a chair.

instrument_label: Stanford Sleepiness Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Stanford Sleepiness Scale Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized single-item self-rated questionnaire, published by Hoddes et al. in 1972, that utilizes a seven-point rating scale to assess subjective sleepiness at any given timepoint.

instrument_label: Stent Thrombosis Coronary ARC Grade Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Stent Thrombosis Coronary ARC Grade Clinical Classification
container_name: ARC STC Grade Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized grading system developed by the Academic Research Consortium (ARC) to describe coronary stent thrombosis based on the evidence (angiographic or pathological confirmation of thrombus, or unexplained sudden death within 30 days) of the event. This grading system includes 3 classifications: definite, probable and possible stent thrombosis.

instrument_label: Satisfaction With Life Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Satisfaction With Life Scale Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Diener et al in 1985, which is used to measure global cognitive judgments of satisfaction with one's life. The questionnaire contains 5 questions and is scored ranging from 1 (Strongly disagree) to 7 (Strongly agree).

instrument_label: Transition Dyspnea Index Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Transition Dyspnea Index Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Mahler et al in 1984, which is a general scale used to measure the changes in dyspnea severity from the baseline as established by the Baseline Dyspnea Index (BDI). The questionnaire contains 3 categories of 24 items change in functional impairment, change in magnitude of task, change in magnitude of effort. The instrument is scored on a seven-grade scale for each category, where grade -3 represents major deterioration and grade +3 represents major improvement.

instrument_label: Coronary Thrombus TIMI Grade Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Coronary Thrombus TIMI Grade Clinical Classification
container_name: TIMI Thrombus Grade Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized grading system developed by the Thrombolysis in Myocardial Infarction (TIMI) Study Group to detect the presence of, and to describe the relative size of coronary thrombus via angiographic assessments.

instrument_label: Trauma Symptom Checklist for Children Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Trauma Symptom Checklist for Children Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 54-item self-report questionnaire developed by Briere et al. in 1996, that utilizes a four-point Likert rating scale to evaluate severity of posttraumatic stress and related symptoms in children ages 10-17 years who have experienced traumatic events.

instrument_label: Trauma Symptom Checklist for Young Children Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Trauma Symptom Checklist for Young Children Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized 90-item caretaker-report questionnaire developed by Briere et al. in 1999, that utilizes a four-point Likert rating scale to evaluate severity of posttraumatic stress and related symptoms in children ages 3-12 years who have experienced traumatic events.

instrument_label: Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale originally developed by S. Fahn, R. Elton, and other members of the UPDRS Development Committee in 1987 which is used to evaluate the status of a patient's Parkinson's disease. This instrument is made up of 6 parts consisting of a mental, behavior and mood section, an activities of daily living section, a motor examination, and questions concerning any complications with the patient's current therapy as well as modified versions of the Hoehn and Yahr Staging assessment and the Schwab and England Activities of Daily Living Scale. Each question is either scored with a 0 for a 'no' response and 1 for a 'yes' response or with a 0-4 point scale.

instrument_label: Movement Disorder Society Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Movement Disorder Society Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed and copyrighted by the Movement Disorders Society in 2008 and published in the journal Movement Disorders (C.G. Goetz et al. Movement Disorder Society-Sponsored Revision of the Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale (MDS-UPDRS): Scale Presentation and Clinimetric Testing Results. Movement Disorders, 23(15):2129-2170.) which is used to evaluate the severity of an individual's Parkinson's disease symptoms. This instrument contains four parts consisting of non-motor and motor experiences in daily living, a motor examination and an assessment of motor complications which is completed during an interview and physical examination. Each symptom is rated on a scale of 0-4 with 0 being normal and 4 being severe.

instrument_label: Urgency Perception Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Urgency Perception Scale Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed and published by Linda Cardozo, Karin Coyne and Eboo Versi in 2004 and copyrighted by Pfizer which is used to assess an individual's sense of urinary urgency. This instrument consists of one item in which the individual rates their experience when they have the desire to urinate. The item has three responses that receive a score of 0, 1, or 2.

instrument_label: Veterans Administration Lung Study Group Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Veterans Administration Lung Study Group Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized two-stage classification system that was developed by the Veterans Administration Lung Study Group (VALG) in 1957 for the clinical staging of lung cancer as limited or extensive based on the anatomical scope of the disease.

instrument_label: Vignos Lower Extremity Rating Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Vignos Lower Extremity Rating Scale Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized 10-item clinician rating scale for the assessment of lower extremity function in patients diagnosed with neuromuscular disease. The scale was developed by Vignos, et al. in 1963 to evaluate ability and methods used for elevation and ambulation in patients with childhood progressive muscular dystrophy.

instrument_label: West Haven Hepatic Encephalopathy Grade Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: West Haven Hepatic Encephalopathy Grade Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Blei and Cordoba in 2001, which is a classification system used to describe the severity of hepatic encephalopathy based on the level of mental status and intellectual function. This instrument has 4 grades.

instrument_label: 4-Stair Ascend Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Four Stair Ascend Test Functional Test
instrument_definition: A timed function test, one of a series of exercise testing modalities that were initially introduced in the 1960s, that measures functional mobility by assessing the time taken and techniques used to climb four standard-size stairs.

instrument_label: Apgar Score V1 Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Apgar Score Version 1 Clinical Classification
container_name: APGAR Score Version 1 Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A scoring system, developed by Dr. Virginia Apgar in 1952, that is used to evaluate the clinical status of the newborn infant by assigning individual scores ranging from zero to two in the assessment of respiratory effort, reflex irritability, muscle tone, heart rate, and color. The cumulative score is reported at one minute after birth, and at five minute intervals up to 20 minutes after birth for infants with a one minute cumulative score of less than seven.

instrument_label: Airway Questionnaire 20 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Airway Questionnaire 20
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Barley et al in 1988 to measure health status and detect changes in quality of life in asthmatic patients. The questionnaire contains 20 questions and each can be answered with Yes, No, or N/A (Not Applicable).

instrument_label: ATLAS Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: ATLAS Score Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized scoring system created by Miller et al. in 2013 that assesses age, treatment with systemic antibiotics, leukocyte count, albumin and serum creatinine to predict treatment response or mortality from Clostridium difficile infection.

instrument_label: Baby Eating Behaviour Questionnaire Concurrent Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Baby Eating Behaviour Questionnaire Concurrent Version
instrument_definition: A standardized 18-item concurrent parent-report questionnaire developed by Llewellyn et al in 2010 as a psychometric measure of infant appetite during the 0-3-month period of exclusive milk-feeding. The instrument, which is derived from the Children's Eating Behaviour Questionnaire, was designed to characterize appetite traits that might confer susceptibility to gain weight.

instrument_label: Baby Eating Behaviour Questionnaire Retrospective Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Baby Eating Behaviour Questionnaire Retrospective Version
instrument_definition: A standardized 18-item retrospective parent-report questionnaire developed by Llewellyn et al in 2010 as a psychometric measure of infant appetite during the 0-3-month period of exclusive milk-feeding. The instrument, which is derived from the Children's Eating Behaviour Questionnaire, was designed to characterize appetite traits that might confer susceptibility to gain weight.

instrument_label: Clinical COPD Questionnaire 24 Hour Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Clinical COPD Questionnaire 24 Hour Version
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale originally developed by Thys van der Molen et al in 2003, which is a self-administered instrument used to measure clinical status of the airways and health-related quality of life in subjects with COPD. The 24 hour version questionnaire contains 10 questions which are divided into 3 areas or domains: symptoms, mental state and functional state.

instrument_label: Clinical COPD Questionnaire 1 Week Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Clinical COPD Questionnaire 1 Week Version
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale originally developed by Thys van der Molen et al in 2003, which is a self-administered instrument used to measure clinical status of the airways and health-related quality of life in subjects with COPD. The 1 week version questionnaire contains 10 questions which are divided into 3 areas or domains: symptoms, mental state and functional state.

instrument_label: CDC Classification System for HIV-Infected Adults and Adolescents Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Center for Disease Control and Prevention Classification System for HIV-Infected Adults and Adolescents Clinical Classification
container_name: CDC Classification System for HIV-Infected Adults and Adolescents Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed and revised by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) in 1993, which is a classification system used to assess the severity of HIV disease based on CD4 cell counts and the presence of specific HIV-related conditions.

instrument_label: Controlled Oral Word Association Test Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Controlled Oral Word Association Test
instrument_definition: A test that measures the verbal fluency of an individual. This tool measures both semantic fluency and phonetic fluency.

instrument_label: Child-Pugh Classification Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Child-Pugh Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale used to assess the severity of liver cirrhosis and determine the prognosis, the required strength of treatment, and the necessity of liver transplantation. This instrument uses the following clinical and lab criteria: encephalopathy grade, ascites, bilirubin, albumin, and prothrombin index.

instrument_label: 4-Stair Descend Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Four Stair Descend Test Functional Test
instrument_definition: A timed function test, one of a series of exercise testing modalities that were initially introduced in the 1960s, that measures functional mobility by assessing the time taken and techniques used to descend four standard-size stairs.

instrument_label: DePaul Symptom Questionnaire - Post Exertional Malaise 2018 Version - 10 Item Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: DePaul Symptom Questionnaire - Post Exertional Malaise 2018 Version - 10 Item
instrument_definition: A standardized 10-item questionnaire developed by Colter et al in 2018 as an updated version of a standardized five-item questionnaire originally developed in 2010 to assess symptoms of post exertional malaise (PEM) in patients with myalgic encephalomyelitis and chronic fatigue (ME/CFS). The updated measure utilizes a five-point rating scale and includes five supplementary items focused on symptom duration and recovery to better distinguish PEM associated with ME/CFS from other fatigue-related conditions.

instrument_label: Emotion Recognition Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Emotion Recognition Test Functional Test
instrument_definition: A standardized method for measuring visibly different facial movements that was developed by Ekman et al in 1976.

instrument_label: European Organization for the Research and Treatment of Cancer Quality of Life Questionnaire - Cholangiocarcinoma and Gallbladder Cancer 21 Items Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: European Organization for the Research and Treatment of Cancer Quality of Life Questionnaire - Cholangiocarcinoma and Gallbladder Cancer 21 Items
instrument_definition: A standardized 21-item questionnaire module developed by the European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer (EORTC) in 2011 that is designed to supplement the EORTC QLQ-C30 core questionnaire in assessing the quality of life in patients with cholangiocarcinoma and cancer of the gallbladder.

instrument_label: Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Cognitive Function Version 3 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: FACT-Cognitive Function
instrument_definition: The FACT-Cognitive Function questionnaire is a 37 item measure developed by Dr. David Cella to evaluate knowledge and information processing.

instrument_label: Revised Fibromyalgia Impact Questionnaire Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Revised Fibromyalgia Impact Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized survey originally developed in the late 1980s and subsequently copyrighted by Robert M Bennett and Ronald Friend and published in the Arthritis Research and Therapy journal (Bennett RM, Friend R, Jones KD, Ward R, Han BK, and Ross RL. The Revised Fibromyalgia Impact Questionnaire (FIQR): validation and psychometric properties. Arthritis Research and Therapy 2009, 11:R120) which is used to evaluate an individual's fibromyalgia symptoms and the extent they interfere with daily living. This instrument is a self-administered questionnaire with 3 sections with a total of 21 items pertaining to the difficulty of performing an activity, the overall impact of fibromyalgia, and the intensity of common fibromyalgia symptoms all within the past 7 days. Each item is ranked on a scale from 0-10.

instrument_label: Health Assessment Questionnaire Disability Index Without Pain Visual Analog Scale Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Health Assessment Questionnaire Disability Index Without Pain Visual Analog Scale
instrument_definition: A standardized 20-item self-report component of the Health Assessment Questionnaire, developed by Fries et al 1978, that assesses a patient's level of functional ability. The disability index utilizes a 4-point symptom rating scale to allow patients to grade eight categories of ability, including dressing and grooming, arising, eating, walking, hygiene, reach, grip, and common daily activities.

instrument_label: International Physical Activity Questionnaire (August 2002) Short Last 7 Days Telephone Format Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: International Physical Activity Questionnaire (August 2002) Short Last 7 Days Telephone Format
container_name: IPAQ Short Last 7 Days Telephone Format Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 9-item questionnaire, developed by Booth et al. in 2000, that is administered over the telephone and assesses physical activity lasting at least 10 minutes during the past seven days.

instrument_label: International Physical Activity Questionnaire (November 2002) Long Last 7 Days Telephone Format Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: International Physical Activity Questionnaire (November 2002) Long Last 7 Days Telephone Format
container_name: IPAQ Long Last 7 Days Telephone Format Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized 31-item questionnaire, developed by Booth et al. in 2000, that is administered over the telephone and assesses physical activity lasting at least 10 minutes during the past seven days.

instrument_label: International Physical Activity Questionnaire (August 2002) Short Last 7 Days Self-Administered Format Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: International Physical Activity Questionnaire (August 2002) Short Last 7 Days Self-Administered Format
container_name: IPAQ Short Last 7 Days Self-administered Format Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized self-administered 9-item questionnaire, developed by Booth et al. in 2000, that assesses physical activity lasting at least 10 minutes during the past seven days.

instrument_label: International Prostate Symptom Score Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: International Prostate Symptom Score
instrument_definition: A screening tool used to screen for, diagnose, track, and manage the symptoms of benign prostatic hyperplasia.

instrument_label: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Symptom Assessment Questionnaire v1.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Symptom Assessment Questionnaire v1.0
instrument_definition: A standardized 7-item patient-reported outcome instrument, created by McCarrier et al. in 2016, that utilizes a 7-day recall period and a five-point verbal rating scale to assess symptoms of non-small cell lung cancer.

instrument_label: The Overactive Bladder Questionnaire Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: The Overactive Bladder Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized survey developed and published by K. Coyne and colleagues in 2002 in the journal Quality of Life Research which is used to evaluate the all symptoms associated with overactive bladder. This instrument contains 33 items pertaining to bladder symptoms occurring during the past week that can be self-administered. Each item is rated on a scale of 1-6 with 1 representing none or not at all and 6 representing a great deal or all the time.

instrument_label: The Overactive Bladder Questionnaire Short Form Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: The Overactive Bladder Questionnaire Short Form
instrument_definition: A standardized survey developed by K. Coyne and colleagues in 2004 and presented at the Joint Meeting of the International Continence Society and the International Urogynecological Association which is used to evaluate the symptoms associated with overactive bladder. This self-administered, shorter version of the Overactive Bladder Questionnaire (OAB-q) contains 19 items pertaining to bladder symptoms occurring during the past four weeks. Each item is rated on a scale of 1-6 with 1 representing none or not at all and 6 representing a great deal or all the time.

instrument_label: PROMIS-29 Profile v2.1 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: PROMIS-29 Profile Version 2.1 Questionnaire
instrument_definition: The first update to the second version of a standardized 29-item measure, published by Cella et al. in 2005, that utilizes 5- and 11-point rating scales to assess pain and health-related quality of life in patient with chronic disease.

instrument_label: Patient Health Questionnaire - 9 Item Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Patient Health Questionnaire - 9 Item
instrument_definition: A 9-item scale using each of the 9 DSM-IV criteria with self-reported frequency of "0" (not at all) to "3" (nearly every day). One of the most widely used instruments to assess depression, PHQ-9 scores of 5, 10, 15, and 20 represent mild, moderate, moderately severe, and severe depression, respectively.

instrument_label: Patient Health Questionnaire - 15 Item Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Patient Health Questionnaire - 15 Item
instrument_definition: A standardized survey developed and published by Kurt Kroenke, Robert Spitzer and Janet Williams in 2002 in the journal Psychosomatic Medicine which is used to assess the severity of an individual's somatic symptoms. This self-administered instrument contains 15 items pertaining to topics such as stomach pain, headaches, dizziness, and trouble sleeping. Each item is rated on a scale of 0-2 with 0 being 'not bothered at all' and 2 being 'bothered a lot.'

instrument_label: Patient Health Questionnaire Screener Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Patient Health Questionnaire Screener Version
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Spitzer et al in 1999, which is used to screen and diagnose mental health disorders of depression, anxiety, alcohol, eating, and somatoform. The screener version of this instrument contains 59 items.

instrument_label: Paced Tapping Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Paced Tapping Test Functional Test
instrument_definition: A cognitive test that is used to assess sensorimotor synchronization and timing of movement, where a subject produces a series of finger taps, at first in time with an external pacing stimulus and then continuing after the stimulus has ended.

instrument_label: Roland Morris Disability Questionnaire Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Roland Morris Disability Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized survey developed by M.O. Roland and R.W. Morris in 1983 used to assess the level of disability experienced by a patient with back pain. This original version consists of 24 statements and can be completed by the patient. Each statement receives either a 0 if it is not applicable to patient on the day the questionnaire is administered or a 1 if it is applicable.

instrument_label: Rising From Floor Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: Rising From Floor Test Functional Test
container_name: Rising From Floor Functional Test Question
instrument_definition: A timed function test created by Araujo et al. (2012) that measures the time taken and techniques utilized to sit and rise from the floor with the minimum amount of support as a predictor of overall health and life expectancy.

instrument_label: Short-Form McGill Pain Questionnaire-2 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Short-Form McGill Pain Questionnaire-2
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed to evaluate the type and severity of a patients neuropathic or non-neuropathic pain. This revised version contains 22 symptoms associated with neuropathic and non-neuropathic pain which can be completed by the patient. Each symptom is rated on a scale of 0-10 with 0 being no pain and 10 being the worst possible pain. This questionnaire was copyrighted by Ronald Melzack and the Initiative on Methods, Measurement, and Pain Assessment in Clinical Trials (IMMPACT) in 2009 as well as published in the journal pain (Dworkin R.H., Development and initial validation of an expanded and revised version of the Short-form McGill Pain Questionnaire-2 (SF-MPQ-2). Pain, 2009 Jul;144(1-2):35-42).

instrument_label: St. George's Respiratory Questionnaire Past 3 Months Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: St. George's Respiratory Questionnaire Past 3 Months Version
container_name: St George's Respiratory Questionnaire Past 3 Months Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized, disease-specific rating scale developed by Jones et al in 1992, which is used to determine the impact on overall health, daily life and perceived well-being in patients with obstructive airway disease. The questionnaire contains 51 items across 2 parts; Symptoms component (frequency and severity with a 3-month performance recall); Impact component (activities that are causes to, or limited by breathlessness).

instrument_label: St. George's Respiratory Questionnaire Past 4 Weeks Version Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: St. George's Respiratory Questionnaire Past 4 Weeks Version
container_name: St George's Respiratory Questionnaire Past 4 Weeks Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A standardized, disease-specific rating scale developed by Jones et al in 1992, which is used to determine the impact on overall health, daily life and perceived well-being in patients with obstructive airway disease. The questionnaire contains 51 items across 2 parts; Symptoms component (frequency and severity with a 4-week performance recall); Impact component (activities that are causes to, or limited by breathlessness).

instrument_label: Symptom Impact Questionnaire Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Symptom Impact Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized survey developed and copyrighted by Ronald Friend and Robert Bennett that is published in the journal Arthritis Research and Therapy. This instrument was developed along with the Revised Fibromyalgia Questionnaire (FIQR) and was intended for use in assessing symptoms associated with rheumatoid arthritis and systemic lupus erythematous. There are three sections including function, overall impact and symptoms with a total of 21 items. Each item is rated on a 0-10 scale with 0 being none and 10 being the most severe.

instrument_label: Tanner Scale Girl Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Tanner Scale Girl Version
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Marshal and Tanner in 1969, which is a classification system used to measure physical development in children, adolescents and adults. The girl version of the tanner scale assesses the different stages of physical maturity based on breast development and the growth of pubic hair. This instrument has 5 stages.

instrument_label: Tanner Scale Boy Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Tanner Scale Boy Version
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by Marshal and Tanner in 1970, which is a classification system used to measure physical development in children, adolescents and adults. The boy version of the tanner scale assesses the different stages of physical maturity based on testicular volume, genitalia growth and the development of pubic hair. This instrument has 5 stages.

instrument_label: 10-Meter Walk/Run Functional Test
instrument_ncit_name: 10-Meter Walk/Run Test Functional Test
instrument_definition: A timed function test, one of a series of exercise testing modalities that were initially introduced in the 1960s, that assesses functional mobility by measuring walking or running speed in meters per second over ten meters.

instrument_label: Treatment Satisfaction Questionnaire for Medication Version 1.4 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Treatment Satisfaction Questionnaire for Medication Version 1.4
instrument_definition: A standardized 14-item self-report scale developed by Atkinson et al in 2004 to assess the major dimensions of patients' satisfaction with medication. The instrument, which permits comparisons across medication types and patient conditions, provides scores on four scales: side effects, effectiveness, convenience, and global satisfaction.

instrument_label: WHO Clinical Staging of HIV/AIDS for Adults and Adolescents Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: World Health Organization Clinical Staging of HIV/AIDS for Adults and Adolescents Clinical Classification
container_name: WHO Clinical Staging of HIV/AIDS for Adults and Adolescents Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by the World Health Organization (WHO) in 2007, which is a classification system used for immunological staging of HIV/AIDS in adults and adolescents with established HIV infection. This instrument classifies HIV disease on the basis of clinical manifestations.

instrument_label: WHO Clinical Staging of HIV/AIDS for Children Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: World Health Organization Clinical Staging of HIV/AIDS for Children Clinical Classification
container_name: WHO Clinical Staging of HIV/AIDS for Children Clinical Classification Question
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by the World Health Organization (WHO) in 2007, which is a classification system used for immunological staging of HIV/AIDS in children with established HIV infection. This instrument classifies HIV disease on the basis of clinical manifestations.

instrument_label: Arizona Sexual Experiences Scale - Female Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Arizona Sexual Experiences Scale-Female Version Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by McGahuey et al in 2000, which is used to measure sexual dysfunction. The female version of the instrument contains 5 items and quantifies sex drive, arousal, vaginal lubrication, ability to reach orgasm, and satisfaction from orgasm.

instrument_label: Arizona Sexual Experiences Scale - Male Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Arizona Sexual Experiences Scale-Male Version Questionnaire
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale developed by McGahuey et al in 2000, which is used to measure sexual dysfunction. The male version of the instrument contains 5 items and quantifies sex drive, arousal, penile erection, ability to reach orgasm, and satisfaction from orgasm.

instrument_label: Brief Psychiatric Rating Scale-Anchored Clinical Classification
instrument_ncit_name: Brief Psychiatric Rating Scale A Clinical Classification
instrument_definition: A standardized rating scale originally developed by Margaret G. Woerner, Salvatore Mannuzza and John M. Kane in 1988 which is used to evaluate the severity of an individual's psychiatric symptoms. This version consists of 18 questions or types of psychiatric symptoms such as depression, anxiety, hallucinations, motor skills, or other unusual behaviors and is completed during a semi-structured interview. Each symptom is rated on a scale of 1-7 or 1-9 with 1 being non-existent and 7 being very severe; 9 represents 'can not be assessed'.

instrument_label: European Organisation for the Research and Treatment of Cancer Quality of Life Questionnaire - Core 30 Version 3.0 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: EORTC Quality of Life Questionnaire-Core 30
instrument_definition: An integrated system for assessing the quality of life of cancer patients participating in clinical trials and other types of research. It incorporates five functional scales: Physical, Role, Cognitive, Emotional, and Social Functioning; three symptom scales: Fatigue, Pain, and Nausea/Vomiting; a Global Health Status/QoL scale; and six single item scales: Dyspnea, Insomnia, Appetite Loss, Constipation, Diarrhea, and Financial Difficulties.

instrument_label: The Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy-Prostate Version 4 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: FACT-P Questionnaire
instrument_definition: The FACT-P is a multidimensional, self-report quality of life instrument specifically designed for use with prostate cancer patients. It consists of 27 core items which assess patient function in four domains: Physical, Social/Family, Emotional, and Functional well-being, which is further supplemented by 12 site specific items to assess for prostate related symptoms (from GEM website https://www.gem-beta.org/public/MeasureDetail.aspx?mid=1074&cat=2)

instrument_label: Functional Assessment of Chronic Illness Therapy-Searchable Item Library Adult Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Functional Assessment of Chronic Illness Therapy-Searchable Item Library Adult Version Questionnaire
container_name: FACIT-Searchable Item Library Adult Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A collection of over 700 items from over 100 Functional Assessment of Chronic Illness Therapy (FACIT) questionnaires that was compiled in 2018 and is used to create custom assessment tools that target a wide range of health-related quality of life issues.

instrument_label: Functional Assessment of Chronic Illness Therapy-Searchable Item Library Pediatric Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Functional Assessment of Chronic Illness Therapy-Searchable Item Library Pediatric Version Questionnaire
container_name: Functional Assessment of Chronic Illness Therapy-Searchable Item Library Pediatric Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A collection of 131 items, developed as part of the Functional Assessment of Chronic Illness Therapy (FACIT) measurement system that was named in 1997 as an expansion of the Functional Assessment of Cancer Therapy (FACT) measures, that is used to create custom assessment tools targeting a wide range of health-related quality of life issues related to pediatric chronic illness.

instrument_label: National Youth Tobacco Survey 2022 Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: National Youth Tobacco Survey 2022 Version Questionnaire
container_name: National Youth Tobacco Survey 2022 Version Questionnaire Question
instrument_definition: A self-administered, electronic school-based survey, distributed as a collaboration between the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Office on Smoking and Health and the Food and Drug Administration, Center for Tobacco Products in 2022, that collected information on tobacco use from students in grades 6 through 12 in all 50 states and the District of Columbia.

instrument_label: Patient Health Questionnaire - 2 Item Questionnaire
instrument_ncit_name: Patient Health Questionnaire-2
instrument_definition: A 2-item scale used to screen for depression and anhedonia over the past 2 weeks. It is comprised of the first 2 items of the Patient Health Questionnaire-9.

instrument_label: Quality Of Life Enjoyment And Satisfaction Questionnaire - Short Form
instrument_ncit_name: Quality of Life Enjoyment and Satisfaction Questionnaire-Short Form
instrument_definition: A standardized 16-item self-report questionnaire that utilizes a 5-point rating scale to assess the degree of enjoyment and satisfaction experienced by adults in various areas of daily functioning. The questionnaire was developed by Endicot et al. in 1993 to assess broad functional impairment in research and treatment settings.

instrument_label: Alzheimer's Disease Assessment Scale-Cognitive 30JUN2015 Functional Test
Alzheimer's Disease Assessment Scale - Cognitive CDISC Version Functional Test
container_name: ADAS-Cog CDISC Version Functional Test Question

instrument_label: Alzheimer's Disease Cooperative Study-Activities of Daily Living Inventory Questionnaire
Alzheimer's Disease Cooperative Study-Activities of Daily Living Inventory Severe Dementia Version Questionnaire
Alzheimer's Disease Cooperative Study-Activities of Daily Living Inventory MCI Version Questionnaire
Alcohol Use Disorder Identification Test: Self-Report Version Questionnaire
Alcohol Use Disorder Identification Test-Consumption Questions Questionnaire
AJCC TNM Staging System 7th Edition Clinical Classification
ADNI Auditory Verbal Learning Functional Test
Rey Auditory Verbal Learning Test Functional Test
Borg CR10 Scale Questionnaire
Craig Handicap Assessment and Reporting Technique - Short Form Interview Version Questionnaire
Columbia-Suicide Severity Rating Scale Baseline/Screening Version Phase 1 Study Questionnaire
